design_philosophy.core_loop: Select fighter from different time periods, compete in tournament bracket, execute character-specific special moves via button combinations or charge mechanics, manage special attack meter resource, position for stage-specific Overkill finishing moves
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (special attack meter depletion vs. recharge cycles) balanced against execution timing and positioning for high-damage Overkills; moral tension between powerful finishing moves and story where all fighters are inherently good
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a historically significant figure from any era (2030 bounty hunter, 1899 Russian acrobat, etc.) to rewrite their fate by winning a supernatural tournament and changing history for the better
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberate stance that all playable characters are morally good or have potential for good—no evil archetypes. Story frames fighting as redemptive rather than destructive. Overkills are gameplay spectacle explicitly decoupled from narrative consequence. Heavy emphasis on character-specific move execution and stage environmental hazards rather than universal combo systems. Built from ground up for home console rather than arcade-to-home port.

difficulty.lives_system: Tournament bracket elimination format; single loss ends current character run
difficulty.checkpointing: Tournament mode progresses through bracket; no mid-match checkpoints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Training mode available with robotic trap defense scenarios for skill practice; controller configuration options (six-button controller or Sega Activator required for full move access; standard three-button controller requires start-button toggling between punch/kick sets)

progression.unlock_mechanism: Beating arcade mode with any character reveals character-specific epilogue showing how victor avoided original death and made positive historical change
replayability_hooks: Nine distinct playable characters each with unique special move command sets
Character-specific endings that reveal different historical outcomes
Different stage environments each with unique Overkill hazard positioning
Multiplayer versus mode for competitive engagement

world_building_techniques: Character profiles established through selection screen and fight intros (name, era, original death circumstance)
Omniscient Eternal Champion framing device: gathers historically important figures moments before their unjust deaths
Character-specific endings as narrative payoff explaining how winner changes their historical fate
Stage environments implicitly tied to character eras and backgrounds
Narrative detail that fighters become allies/friends during tournament (revealed in some endings)

boss_design.philosophy: Tournament bracket structure with escalating difficulty; final opponent determined by tournament seeding. AI opponent can perform special moves even after meter depletion (exploiting rule asymmetry vs. player constraint). Stage-specific hazards create environmental threat layer alongside opponent

faction_indicators: Time period origin (1899 Russian aristocracy, 2030 future bounty hunter, etc.)
Character class/profession (circus acrobat, bounty hunter, revolutionary figure implied)
Moral framework: all characters selected for inherent goodness or redemptive potential
danger_signals: Overkill finishing moves stage-triggered environmental hazards
Special attack meter depletion creates vulnerability windows
Dizzy state after hit combos creates defenseless openings
environmental_storytelling: Stage hazards tied to character backgrounds and eras
Overkill stage-specific deaths reflect environmental narrative (background elements kill defeated opponent)
Character-specific intros and win poses establish personality and historical context
